item.title: Tai Chi for Health and Balance
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Tai Chi is a health sustaining exercise and a tool for improved body awareness. Practiced by the elderly for balance and circulation and the young for relaxation and martial arts, it is a wonderful holistic activity for all ages. We'll focus on learning the entire Yang short form, and you'll receive guidance in developing your own regular practice at home. During this class, you'll learn: -The Yang Tai Chi short form, which is comprised of 35 individual movements -Chi Kung, a warm-up exercise and relaxation activity with 18 movements -Elements of Chinese Medical Philosophy, such as Yin/Yang theory and Five Elements transformation -the basics of Taoist philosophy Learning Objectives Learn basic warm-up stretches to improve your overall movement Discover the Yang-style short form of Tai Chi Practice the Yang-style under the direction of an expert instructor Gain knowledge about the history and background of Tai Chi Who Might Enjoy This Class? Individuals seeking a low-impact exercise routine Anyone interested in improving their coordination and balance People interested in developing their focus, relaxation and overall wellness

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

First Thursdays in the Native Plant Nursery! Calling all n ative plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us every first Th ursday of each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nu rsery. Every year we host a couple thousand native plants in the nursery u ntil they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great opportunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted pla nts, up-potting or re-potting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, a nd watering. When: Thursdays, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants , boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB

item.title: Earth Month Event | Restoration at Ellis Pond
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Celebrate Earth Month at our Ellis Pond Work Party Join us at Ellis Pond Park for a Forest-Steward led weeding and clean-up event fo r Earth Month. Removing certain plants will help build even more wildlife habitat in this small neighborhood park teeming with birds and other woodl and creatures. We will remove weeds like English ivy, Himalayan blackberry, and English holly to help protect native plants and forest health. Come join friends and neighbors in this fun Saturday event. No experie nce necessary; gloves and tools are provided. Participation is limited, so please register. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a parent or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 ki ds. When: Saturday, April 11th, 9:30am-11:30pm Where: Ellis Pond Park \(meet at the bench next to the boardwalk on 90th Ave SE\) What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: lon g pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring a water bottle. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. And a snack or dish to share. Registration : volunteersignup.org/Y8XQX

Event details

item.title: Foraging for Basketry and Wild Crafts
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by basket maker and teacher Erin Cox. Join the conversation about the incredible plants that grow here in the Pacific Northwest and explore ways to identify, gather, store and use them to create baskets and crafts that bring nature inside. Whether you live in an urban, suburban or rural setting, there are forage-able crafting and weaving materials to be found. Daylily, daffodil, dandelion, crocosmia, iris, cattails, juncus, bulrush, ivy, honeysuckle, wisteria, dogwood, willow and more! Erin will share about her approach to sustainable and responsible foraging. We should all care for the plants we harvest and preserve the environments they grow in. This discussion is good for beginners and you will walk away with some fun, easy ways to get started. $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member Erin found her life’s passion in basketry and wild crafting. In 2024 she established a small basketry willow farm in Carnation, Washington, where she invites students to learn, harvest, and engage with the materials. In 2025, she opened Field and Forest Crafts and her own private teaching studio in NE Seattle.

Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 9 AM

The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: Spy Maters Camp II
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the link to book a spot!

item.title: Global Soccer Game Webinar
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Virtually via zoom, This event will be recorded and distributed after the event to all those who register., Tukwila, WA

Join Us for the FIFA Ready Webinar Date: February 10th, 2:30 PM Location: Virtually Via Zoom Event Overview: As we gear up for the monumental FIFA events, it&'s crucial to align our regional efforts to maximize economic and infrastructural benefits. This meeting will gather key stakeholders from various sectors, including our cities and business community, to strategize and synchronize our readiness plans with an emphasis in preparing our business community to ensure you are ready for this economic opportunity. So do you want to learn about what resources are available to you to maximize this time? Register to learn more now. These quarterly events are brought to you by the Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce and Explore Seattle Southside. This meeting is a pivotal opportunity for stakeholders to collaborate and ensure our region is fully prepared to host FIFA events successfully. Together, we can create a unified and effective execution plan that benefits all.

item.title: Audubon Bird Walk
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Learn more about the birds at the Bellevue Botanical Garden. Join Eastside Audubon for a monthly walk that explores the birds and their habitat in the Garden. This one-mile walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. Parking in the lot is free. From the parking lot enter the garden through the breezeway and turn left. Meet your guide in the covered Gathering Place. This walk is for any level of birder and no experience is necessary. Bring binoculars if you have them. There will be some available to loan for the walk. Each walk is $5 per person. Registration is required. Sorry, the June walk is full. View our calendar to register for upcoming walks.

item.title: Earth Month Event | Nature Journaling
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 10 AM

Nature Journaling in Pioneer Park It's Earth Month! And to celebrate we're hosting the first of our seasonal Nature Journaling educa tional events. We will be exploring Pioneer Park with notebooks and pens m aking observations about what we see. Participants will get instructions o n how to begin nature journaling as well as a series of prompts to help gu ide your observations. What is nature journaling? It is a practice in which you observe nature and spend some time crafting notes whether that' s words, drawings, painting, quantitative data, etc. Click here to learn more: https://johnmuirlaws.com/nature-journaling-starting-growing / All ages welcome, no experience necessary. When: Thursday, April 16th, 10am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dr ess for the weather in comfortable clothes. Please bring a notebook, and pens/pencils/water colors/crayons/etc Registration: https://volunteersig nup.org/J7KYK

item.title: Forterra Fridays: April 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Duwamish Hill Preserve, 3800 South 115th Street, Tukwila, WA

Forterra Fridays: April 2026 Friday, Apr. 17, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We’re excited to invite you to volunteer with us for a special Earth Month event at Duwamish Hill Preserve, a Tukwila park providing critical wildlife habitat and public trails in the middle of an industrial area. Join us on Friday, April 17th, from 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm, to restore essential wildlife habitat by removing Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plants benefit local wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality.  You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation. We’ll start with a walk of the site and Earth Day-themed trivia and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and the Green Tukwila Partnership and Forterra.  What we are doing: The Preserve now includes an outdoor classroom area, the Cultural Garden, an enhanced trail system, and continued restoration opportunities for volunteers. We’ll be removing the blackberry to ensure the trees, shrubs, and groundcover plants can thrive.   It’s a great opportunity to learn about Forterra’s innovative approach to land conservation, including its role in safeguarding the Duwamish Hill Preserve.  What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the nearby parking lot at Duwamish Hill Preserve. Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet.  Date and Time: Friday, April 17, 2026, 1:00 –3:30 PM  Register and learn more here.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Pilates for Gardeners
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Jo Anne Iaciofano. Grab your mat, kick off your shoes, and join this Pilates-inspired class to improve body movement, core strength, and muscle control. This beginner’s friendly class, using mind-body connection and breath, will take you through a series of exercises with an emphasis on technique and fluidity. We will focus on those areas most stressed during gardening by using your core to power the muscles increasing your energy and endurance. All levels are welcome. Wear comfortable clothing and be prepared to workout barefoot. Please bring a yoga or Pilates mat, towel, and water. This class will take place in the Aaron Education Center.  $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member.

Event details

item.title: Green Issaquah: Restoration Event at Park Pointe
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: SE Evans St, Issaquah, WA

Join the Issaquah Alps Trails Club and Green Issaquah on the third Saturday of each month for invasive species removal events at Park Pointe! We will be removing invasive species such as Himalayan blackberry bushes to prepare for our fall planting event. These invasive plants inhibit the growth of native plants, and removing them will ensure the longevity and health of our newest additions. This event is part of our ongoing efforts to help meet the Issaquah Climate Action Plan goal of 55% tree canopy cover by 2035. When and Where Date & Time: Saturday, April 18th, 2025, 9 AM - Noon Where: Park Pointe Where to Park Parking for Park Pointe is available along SE Evans Street, which is overflow for Issaquah High School and the parking for the Sportsman Club. Where to Meet We will be meeting at the end of the dirt road at the trailhead. What to Bring Bring your own gloves if you have them. There will be gloves available for those who don't have their own. Please dress in layers and in clothing you don't mind getting dirty. Closed-toed shoes are required. Long sleeves and pants, especially denim, are strongly recommended. Bring a full water bottle and snacks. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen! Point of Contact Anne Newcomb \(anne@annenewcomb.net\) and Matt LaBelle \(mattlabelle@hotmail.com\), your work party leaders.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

Event details

item.title: GU Earth Day Volunteering
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park, Issaquah, WA

Zags—In honor of Earth Day, let’s get outside and give back! Join the Seattle Chapter for a community service day at Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park in Sammamish on April 18, 10 AM–2 PM. We’ll be teaming up with Evergreen Mountain Bike Alliance to do trail maintenance at one of the region’s most-loved riding spots. No experience needed—just bring your energy, your Zag spirit, and a pair of work gloves. All tools will be provided by Evergreen, and we’ll have pizza and drinks ready for everyone. Kids are welcomed! Spots are limited—sign up here: https://www.evergreenmtb.org/index.php?option=com\_civicrm&task=civicrm/event/info&reset=1&id=5306 Let’s make an impact together. Go Zags!

item.title: South Lynnwood Park Stewardship Event
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Scriber Lake Park, 5562 196th St SW, Lynnwood, WA

City of Lynnwood Parks welcomes volunteers of all abilities who want to contribute to the restoration and conservation of our parks in South Lynnwood Neighborhood. The goal of each event is to provide a safe, enjoyable, and effective hands-on experience for you to help improve the health of your urban parks. These monthly events, which will be held on the third weekend of each month, will involve removing invasive plants, help with restoration plantings, and picking up garbage. Events are 100% outdoors and will generally be held ‘rain or shine’ but may be canceled or relocated if weather or site conditions are unsafe such as high winds, flooding, snow, lightning, or unsafe air quality \(smoke\).

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th \(37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend, starting at 9:00 AM\) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center \(5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle, WA 98199\) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group, please email communications@unitedindians.org

item.title: Earth Month Event | Plant ID Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 1 PM

Earth Month Plant ID Walk Spring is in bloom, and we're exited to go explore Pioneer Park looking for our native flowers. Join us w e walk through the northeast quadrant, identifying native plants, sharing plant identification tips, and sharing about the different roles plants play in maintain healthy ecosystems. All ages welcome, no experience necessary. When: Tuesday, April 21st, 1-3pm Where: Pioneer Park NE, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you're comfo rtable walking in. You might want to bring a note book for notetaking, or a camera to take pictures of the flowers! Registration: https://volunte ersignup.org/J7KYK

item.title: Stifel Stars on Ice Pre-Show Stargazer Pass
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Climate Pledge Arena, 334 1st Ave N, Seattle, WA

THIS IS AN OPTIONAL UPGRADE. TICKET TO THE MAIN EVENT IS ALSO REQUIRED. Pre-Show Stargazer Event takes place prior to Stars on Ice show. Enjoy early entry, watch the skater warm-ups, and participate in a Q&A with several skaters from specified seating sections. Due to this event taking place prior to doors opening, EVERYONE entering the venue early must have an event ticket AND Pre-Show Stargazer Pass for entry. This includes all children as well as the parent(s)/guardian(s) wishing to accompany them into the building. If you plan on attending the Pre-Show Stargazer Event, please make sure you have BOTH tickets. NO EXCEPTIONS. Late arrivals will not be refunded. OPTIONAL UPSELL. NOT VALID FOR EVENT ADMISSION. MUST ALSO HAVE EVENT TICKET! FAN MUST BRING UPSELL TICKET TO EVENT DAY FOR ENTRY. NOTE SPECIFIC PRE-SHOW ACCESS START TIME! STARGAZER PASS TICKET REQUIRED FOR ENTRY.

From Climate Pledge Arena
